player_utterance: Quiet night for it, isn't it.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup in one hand, the other in his coat pocket

player_utterance: I knew him the way you know someone at the edges — a few conversations, a handshake or two. Enough to want to be here.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, looking down at the cold coffee, then back up

player_utterance: What kind of man was he to you?

believer_state_after.posture: standing, eyes on the candle on the small table, weight shifting from one foot to the other
standing still, eyes on the player, then drifting to the candle flame

player_utterance: He sat down like a man who finally gave himself permission. That tells me something about what he was carrying in there.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands clasped between his knees, looking at the player sideways

player_utterance: You don't have to answer fast. I'm not in a hurry.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, looking at his hands, one thumb working against the other palm

voice_offers.read: He flinched at 'permission.' Press the word.
voice_offers.line: Permission from what, John? You taught him for thirty years. You'd know what he was asking to put down.

voice_offers.read: He's sideways, not gone. Let him find the word.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to answer fast. I'm not in a hurry.

player_utterance: Whatever it is, you don't have to say it cleanly.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the folded program on his knee, one hand flat over it

voice_offers.read: Silence twice now; he'll harden if we keep coasting.
voice_offers.line: His hands won't stop working. What did he come to you to be forgiven for, John?

voice_offers.read: He's working something in his hands; let the quiet hold.
voice_offers.line: Whatever it is, you don't have to say it cleanly.

player_utterance: You've been holding that down all morning.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, both hands flat on the program now, looking down

voice_offers.read: Two soft passes given; the silence is now doing his work for him.
voice_offers.line: You keep that program flat under your hand like you're holding something down. What's under it, John?

voice_offers.read: He teaches the Lutheran texts; ask him what he actually told the man.
voice_offers.line: When he came to you, what did you tell him? Not the comfort — the doctrine. Word for word.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting, hands lifting from the program on his knee, turning it over to the blank side

voice_offers.read: Three gentle openings; a fourth would crowd him.
He's looking down at his own hands; press the gesture.
voice_offers.line: Both hands on it now. What are you keeping from lifting off that page, John?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting, looking down at the program flipped face-down on his knee, one finger resting on its edge

voice_offers.read: He turned it blank himself; the silence is working.
He flipped the page — now the question lands clean.
voice_offers.line: You turned it over yourself, John. What's on the side you won't read?

player_utterance: Whatever you need that blank side to be right now, that's alright.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting, hand still resting on the edge of the program, shoulders lowered

voice_offers.read: He flipped it himself; the wound is already open.
voice_offers.line: Read me what's on that side, John. Out loud.

voice_offers.read: His finger rests there like a question he can't ask.
voice_offers.line: Whatever it says — you don't have to read it to me. Just tell me his name.

player_utterance: His name. That's all — you don't owe me the rest.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting, eyes on the candle, hand drifting toward the cold coffee without picking it up

voice_offers.read: He needs permission to grieve, not a pried-open confession.
voice_offers.line: His name. That's all. You don't owe me the rest.

voice_offers.read: He's softened; one true question lands now or never.
voice_offers.line: Who was he to you, John?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting, looking at the candle, both hands open on his knees now

voice_offers.read: He's gathering himself; the question is asked, let it land.
Three silences in. Name him or the moment closes.

player_utterance: That's what Ray deserved today. Someone who just sat.

believer_state_after.posture: leaning forward, elbows on knees, head bowed, one hand at the back of his neck

voice_offers.read: Silence held him; do not break what's working.
He's open-handed and undone; one quiet word lands now.
voice_offers.line: Just his name, John. Whenever you can.

believer_state_after.posture: still leaning forward, but slowly straightening — one hand reaching for the program on his knee, turning it over face-up again

voice_offers.read: He's already there; the silence is working, don't crowd it.
He's bowed; one quiet question now and the name comes.
voice_offers.line: Whose name are you holding, John?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting, program face-up on his knee, looking at Ray's photograph

voice_offers.read: He's reaching for the program himself; let him arrive.
He's straightening — the silence has done its work, now a name.
voice_offers.line: Read me the name on the program, John.
